Naturally, while this led to the end of talks with the Prussian governments, our allies in Poznan have formally asked for us to assist them in liberating Greater Poland, letting them finally join our state!

\n\nAs liberal agitation spread into Europe, revolutionaries in Hungary have formally taken control over once Austrian-dominated kingdom, with the revolutionary Lajos Kossuth at the head of the Hungarian National Defense Commission, currently ruling the precarious country. The "Hungarian Cause" has long interested the more revolutionary-inclined elements in our government, with many in our current cabinet seeing Hungary as a brotherly nation in the struggle against foreign powers. At this point, we are under heavy pressure from the Sejm and our own cabinet to rally behind Hungary and help them liberate themselves from Habsburg rule!

\n\nWhile we always suspicious of how the newly-independent Polish state could threaten our domains, we could never imagine that they would be so bold as to openly support our rebellious subjects at the first opportunity. Now, with revolts spreading in our territory, the Polish have officially shown support to our enemies, leaving it clear that they would be willing to declare war on us to segure the liberal revolutions that spread across our country!

\n\nAs we made an effort to coordinate the efforts of the Polish elites and revolutionaries abroad, now that we cross into enemy territory we are greated by the local Polish population as liberators, with our allies in their lands quickily taking up arms and declaring their allegiance to our state. With their support, we are sure to destroy our enemies in the field of battle!
The Prussian Threat

§RGain 5 infamy§! While we already have an interest in parts of East Prussia—such as Masuria—due to their Polish minority, the most important part of the region—Königsberg—has long been out of our ambitions, with it—along with most of the Prussian province of East Prussia—having been part of Ducal Prussia, with the region being a fiefdom of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth for over 100 years. \n\nMore recently, Prince Adam Jerzy Czartoryski—while withdrawn from politics after his father's death—published his book "Essay on Diplomacy", where the former Foreign Minister of Tsar Alexander I would urge Russia to access the Prussian threat and incorporate East Prussia into a ressurgent Poland. While these ideas wouldn't come to fruition with the November Uprising breaking later in that year, the Prussian threat that once loomed over Russia now looms over our fragile Polish state, with many in our cabinet revisiting Czartoryski's ideas and suggesting that we claim East Prussia to completely secure what otherwise would be an exclave that Prussia would be keen on connecting with.
Renounce National Egoism

§GLose 5 infamy§! §RThis will remove German cores on Poznan and Thorn!§! When the Poznan Question was brought into the Frankfurt Parliament, Polish Independence was still supported by many representatives that tried to reconcile German and Polish Nationalism. As our attempts to divide Poznan failed to find a settlement that wouldn't leave neither Germans or Germans stranded outside or their territory, those ideas would be ultimately killed by Wilhelm Jordan's speech, where the liberal parliamentarian would paint the  "sentimental Polonophilia" of some liberals as a childish fantasy that had no right to get in the way of a unified Germany and its future, stating that the Germans should practice "healthy national egoism" and abandon any sympathy for the Polish cause within Poznan. Jordan's speech would erode most support for Polish independence within the German liberals, with only radicals like Robert Blum insisting on advocating for an independent Poland. \n\nNow, while we did promote a violent crackdown in Greater Poland after our cooperation with the Polish became unsustainable, many of the radical liberal and socialist elements in our country still favorably see Polish independence and there are still some that see the establishment of Poland as pivotal to curtail a future Russian threat. While it obviously would be madness to establish a Polish state in Poznan, if we were to conquer Warsaw we could leverage the liberal and socialist members of our parliament to renounce our national egoism and push a semi-independent Polish nation that would—under our supervision—fulfill both the interests of both the German and Polish peoples.

\n\nHistorically, the the noble rulers of Brandenburg have exploited Prussia's position to elevate themselves to kings during the times of the Holy Roman Empire, but now, due to recent losses, we have lost control over the territory of Prussia. While we could keep claiming the name of Prussia—as it became synonymous with our domains—it would be dishonest to keep claiming the name of Prussia, especially considering we aren't under the same restrictions we were during the times of the Holy Roman Empire. With our humiliating loss of Prussia, we are forced to recognize that Brandenburg was always our power base.
